why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:resource_pack_push must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a body-consuming resource_pack_push decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for an official no-prompt fixture only and is separate from resource-pack UI behavior, pack download/reload/application behavior, Configuration completion, Play entry, world load, render readiness, or runtime behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_resource_pack_push_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 9, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x09.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ClientboundResourcePackPushPacket(UUID, String, String, boolean, Optional<Component>), ClientboundResourcePackPushPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundResourcePackPushPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundResourcePackPushPacket field accessors.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound resource_pack_push dispatch/decode.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound mapping contains resource_pack_pop id 0x08, but not resource_pack_push id 0x09.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0x9 in Clientbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration clientbound resource_pack_push packet id 9
